title: chamaesyce bartolomei
intro: Chamaesyce bartolomei, also known as the Hawaiian sandmat, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family that is native to Hawaii. The medicinal properties of Chamaesyce bartolomei lie in its leaves and stems, which have been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and fight off infections, providing health benefits such as wound healing and improved immune function. Chamaesyce bartolomei can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
